### Post by quux on 2006-11-04
Did you install the "nvidia-glx" (and the nvidia driver packages) before?

---

### Post by pay on 2006-11-04
What video card do you have? if it is a legacy video card (older card) then you need the legacy drivers.

---

### Post by tommy1987 on 2006-11-04
okay, here is the details:

I did these commands to install it, have tried straight nvidia-glx before and didnt work so using legacy drivers:

$sudo aptitude install nvidia-glx-legacy nvidia-kernel-common nvidia-xconfig linux-restricted-modules-`uname -r`
 
$sudo nvidia-xconfig

Im using dapper as well.

..yes its a Geforce 5600

### Post by tommy1987 on 2006-11-04

### Post by Dual Cortex on 2006-11-04
They are supposed to. What is the problem, X is not loading up?
Install the DRIVERS you are supposed to install, if X doesn't load up, press CTRL+ALT+F1 at the same time, then log in and type:
```
sudo dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg
```

---

### Post by blendmaster on 2006-11-04
Have you tried using [Automatix]("http://getautomatix.com/wiki/index.php?title=Installation&Itemid=38") to install the drivers?

It usually works quite well...
